title: podman run MIGHT not be using the right credentials
body:
## Description

While using `podman-compose` or just `podman` to build an image it finally fails trying to pull the created image.

## Steps to reproduce the issue (see containers/buildah#2426):

```shell
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ flush_containers --prune
==> Stopping and removing pods (if any)...

==> Stopping and removing containers (if any)...

==> Destroying the world...
Deleted Pods
Deleted Containers
Deleted Volumes
Deleted Images

==> Results
REPOSITORY  TAG     IMAGE ID  CREATED  SIZE

POD ID  NAME    STATUS  CREATED  NAMES   INFRA ID

CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES


[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ buildah build-using-dockerfile --tag acme/erlang-cowboy:latest ./ 
STEP 1: FROM docker.io/library/erlang:23.0.2-alpine AS builder
Getting image source signatures

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

STEP 3: COPY . ./
STEP 4: RUN set -eux;   mkdir release/;   rebar3 as prod tar;   tar -zxf _build/prod/rel/erlang_cowboy/erlang_cowboy-*.tar.gz -C /tmp/workspace/release/

+ rebar3 as prod tar

denied: requested access to the resource is denied
unauthorized: authentication required



[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ podman run --detach --name erlang-cowboy --publish 9080:9080 --tty acme/erlang-cowboy:latest 
416cf4ad893dc9346a1aa4f1b707017c0c1b5d774c7dafb04e54df76ae2c0bdf
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ podman images 
REPOSITORY                    TAG            IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy  latest         c43ff5126d15  25 seconds ago  17 MB
docker.io/library/erlang      23.0.2-alpine  7578713ee0f1  4 weeks ago     70.9 MB
docker.io/library/alpine      3.11           f70734b6a266  2 months ago    5.88 MB
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ podman ps --all 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                COMMAND     CREATED         STATUS             PORTS                   NAMES
416cf4ad893d  localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy:latest  foreground  15 seconds ago  Up 14 seconds ago  0.0.0.0:9080->9080/tcp  erlang-cowboy
```

---

This is how my `registries.conf` file looks like:

```shell
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ cat ~/.config/containers/registries.conf 
# The only valid categories are: 'registries.search', 'registries.insecure',
# and 'registries.block'.

[registries.search]
registries = ["quay.io", "docker.io"]

[registries.insecure]
registries = []

#
# Docker only
[registries.block]
registries = []
```

## Describe the results you received:

`podman` fails to run/pull the image.

## Describe the results you expected:

To be able to find and pull the image from local registry.

## Output of `buildah version`:

```shell
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ buildah version
Version:         1.15.0
Go Version:      go1.14.4
Image Spec:      1.0.1-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  v0.7.2-0.20190904153231-83439463f784
image Version:   5.5.1
Git Commit:      unknown
Built:           Wed Dec 31 19:00:01 1969
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

## Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:

```shell
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ podman version
Version:      2.0.1
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.4
Built:        Wed Dec 31 19:00:01 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

## Output of `cat /etc/*release`:

```shell
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ cat /etc/*release
NAME="Arch Linux"
PRETTY_NAME="Arch Linux"
ID=arch
BUILD_ID=rolling
ANSI_COLOR="38;2;23;147;209"
HOME_URL="https://www.archlinux.org/"
DOCUMENTATION_URL="https://wiki.archlinux.org/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://bbs.archlinux.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.archlinux.org/"
LOGO=archlinux
```

## Output of `uname -a`:

```shell
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ uname -a
Linux uplink 5.4.48-1-lts containers/buildah#1 SMP Mon, 22 Jun 2020 08:10:21 +0000 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

## Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:

I don't have that file :innocent:

comments.body:
Now I see why it's not working.

```
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ podman images 
REPOSITORY                    TAG            IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy  latest         c43ff5126d15  25 seconds ago  17 MB
```
^^^ as `build bud --tag` refers to an image without host, it's getting prefixed with `localhost/` but podman does not look for that.

@baude @rhatdan @mtrmac ... I think we should also look for a normalized reference _before_ pulling. WDYT?

Update: scratch that. `podman run` behaves correctly.

>  podman pull acme/erlang-cowboy:latest 

That doesn't really make sense for a local image. `pull` really wants to pull an image from a registry
In the other issue, you mentioned that could not `podman run` this image which would be a problem.
What happens if you do a `podman pull localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy `?

> What happens if you do a `podman pull localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy `?

That will reach out to `localhost` and think it's a registry. `podman pull containers-storage:localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy ` would "work"

If I build the image with `buildah`, I can do `poman run`~~, but not `podman pull` — probably because what you said about the local image~~. I was using initially `podman-compose up --build --detach`. That's what I was testing from the beginning. Probably I mixed things while answering some other questions.

Bottom line, all for the same project:

- `podman-compose up --build --detach` does not work
- `buildah build-using-dockerfile --tag acme/erlang-cowboy:latest ./` and then `podman run --detach --name erlang-cowboy --publish 9080:9080 --tty acme/erlang-cowboy:latest` works (which I what I would expect that `docker-compose` would do behind scenes...or something similar)
- ~~`podman pull acme/erlang-cowboy:latest` does not work~~

> `podman pull` works, but it must be in this form: `podman pull containers-storage:localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy`

---

Log from `podman-compose`:

```shell
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ podman-compose up --build --detach 
podman build -t acme/erlang-cowboy:latest -f ./Dockerfile .

Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: WORKDIR /tmp/workspace/

STEP 3: COPY . ./
--> 3821d4d663d
STEP 4: RUN set -eux;   mkdir release/;   rebar3 as prod tar;   tar -zxf _build/prod/rel/erlang_cowboy/erlang_cowboy-*.tar.gz -C /tmp/workspace/release/
ERRO[0000] systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available 
systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/sh -c set -eux;   mkdir release/;   rebar3 as prod tar;   tar -zxf _build/prod/rel/erlang_cowboy/erlang_cowboy-*.tar.gz -C /tmp/workspace/release/]: : exit status 1
Error: error building at STEP "RUN set -eux;   mkdir release/;   rebar3 as prod tar;   tar -zxf _build/prod/rel/erlang_cowboy/erlang_cowboy-*.tar.gz -C /tmp/workspace/release/": error while running runtime: exit status 1
125
podman pod create --name=erlang_cowboy --share net -p 9080:9080
62b39104c83b6a8868edb0a399cc21971884dd3477b8bf30e7ef2825c9a830cd
0
podman run --name=erlang-cowboy -d --pod=erlang_cowboy -l io.podman.compose.config-hash=123 -l io.podman.compose.project=erlang_cowboy -l io.podman.compose.version=0.0.1 -l com.docker.compose.container-number=1 -l com.docker.compose.service=api --add-host api:127.0.0.1 --add-host erlang-cowboy:127.0.0.1 acme/erlang-cowboy:latest
Trying to pull quay.io/acme/erlang-cowboy:latest...

Trying to pull docker.io/acme/erlang-cowboy:latest...
  denied: requested access to the resource is denied
Error: unable to pull acme/erlang-cowboy:latest: 2 errors occurred:
  * Error initializing source docker://quay.io/acme/erlang-cowboy:latest: Error reading manifest latest in quay.io/acme/erlang-cowboy: unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized
  * Error initializing source docker://acme/erlang-cowboy:latest: Error reading manifest latest in docker.io/acme/erlang-cowboy: errors:
denied: requested access to the resource is denied
```

> The issue that you are stating might be a problem, but still, I see this before: `ERRO[0000] systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available`.

> That will reach out to `localhost` and think it's a registry. `podman pull containers-storage:localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy ` would "work"

```shell
[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ podman images 
REPOSITORY                    TAG            IMAGE ID      CREATED        SIZE
localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy  latest         9f9acabf107b  7 seconds ago  17 MB
docker.io/library/erlang      23.0.2-alpine  7578713ee0f1  4 weeks ago    70.9 MB
docker.io/library/alpine      3.11           f70734b6a266  2 months ago   5.88 MB

[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ podman ps --all 
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES

[x80486@archbook:~/Workshop/Development/erlang_cowboy]$ podman pull containers-storage:localhost/acme/erlang-cowboy 
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b3406a7b0601 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 3e207b409db3 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 9f9acabf10 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
9f9acabf107bd676e0588ba6f8eee28e5fa0e5e475c09668556a4487b9ad982d
```
Curious. The `podman-compose` logs suggests that the build failed and yet it continues and wants to run the image (which doesn't exist).

The pull errors are a red herring. `podman-compose` shouldn't continue if the build failed.

To the build error:

> ERRO[0000] systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available 
systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available

Circling back to @giuseppe for this one.

> Curious. The `podman-compose` logs suggests that the build failed and yet it continues and wants to run the image (which doesn't exist).

Once we figured out what's going on, I suggest to open an issues at podman-compose for this one.

> To the build error:
> 
> > ERRO[0000] systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available
> > systemd cgroup flag passed, but systemd support for managing cgroups is not available
> 
> Circling back to @giuseppe for this one.

try with --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs.  Looks like there is no systemd available but the default is still to use it.

OK, since I'm using the `podman-compose` command, where should I put that argument/setting? If I do `podman-compose up --build --detach --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs` it tells me `podman-compose: error: unrecognized arguments: --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs`.
Now as we figured that out, I suggest moving the issue to `podman-compose`. It's a separate project by the community.
